agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Digital wallet platform enabling money transfers between users, subject to regulatory compliance (KYC/AML), fraud prevention, and transaction integrity controls. The product operates in a regulated financial services space where identity verification tiers govern account capabilities and transfer limits.","bounded_context":"Digital Wallet — Account Lifecycle & Transaction Processing","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"KYC (Know Your Customer)","definition":"Identity verification process that determines an account's capability tier. Completion unlocks full send/withdraw privileges."},{"term":"Unverified Account","definition":"An account that has not begun or completed any identity verification. Can receive funds only — cannot send or withdraw."},{"term":"Partially-Verified Account","definition":"An account that has completed some but not all KYC steps. Implied to have limited send capability with a $500 daily transfer limit, though exact capabilities are ambiguous in the spec."},{"term":"Verified Account","definition":"An account that has fully completed KYC. Has full send/withdraw/receive capabilities with a $5,000 daily transfer limit."},{"term":"Daily Transfer Limit","definition":"Maximum aggregate outbound transfer amount within a rolling or calendar day, determined by account verification tier."},{"term":"Settlement (T+1)","definition":"The point at which a transaction becomes final and irreversible — one business day after initiation."},{"term":"Sanctioned Entity","definition":"A person, organization, or jurisdiction on a regulatory sanctions list (e.g., OFAC). All transfers involving sanctioned entities must be blocked."},{"term":"Account Lock","definition":"A security state triggered by 5 consecutive failed login attempts. Locked accounts cannot authenticate until a support agent manually unlocks them."},{"term":"High-Value Transaction Notification","definition":"An alert sent to the user for any single transaction exceeding $1,000."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Users must complete KYC (identity verification) before they can send money.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Regulatory requirement — allowing unverified users to send funds violates AML/KYC regulations and exposes the platform to legal liability. System integrity depends on this gate.","testable_assertion":"An account with verification_status = 'unverified' must be rejected from initiating any outbound transfer."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Unverified accounts can receive money but cannot send or withdraw.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Receiving is low-risk from a regulatory standpoint, but sending/withdrawing without identity verification is a compliance violation. This is a hard regulatory gate.","testable_assertion":"An inbound transfer to an unverified account must succeed; an outbound transfer or withdrawal from an unverified account must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Daily transfer limit is $5,000 for verified accounts.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Limit thresholds are business policy — a VP or compliance officer could adjust this number based on risk appetite or market conditions.","testable_assertion":"The sum of all outbound transfers for a verified account within one day must not exceed $5,000. Any transfer that would breach this limit must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Daily transfer limit is $500 for partially-verified accounts.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Policy-driven threshold, adjustable by business decision. The existence of the tier itself is policy, not a physical or integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"The sum of all outbound transfers for a partially-verified account within one day must not exceed $500. Any transfer that would breach this limit must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"A transaction cannot be reversed once it has been settled (T+1).","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Settlement finality is a core financial integrity invariant. Reversing settled transactions would corrupt ledger state and violate accounting principles.","testable_assertion":"Any reversal or chargeback request for a transaction with status = 'settled' must be rejected by the system. Only pre-settlement transactions may be reversed."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Transfers to sanctioned entities must be blocked automatically before execution.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Legal/regulatory mandate — processing a transfer to a sanctioned entity is a criminal offense. This can never be overridden by any business policy.","testable_assertion":"Before any transfer is executed, the recipient must be screened against the sanctions list. If matched, the transfer must be blocked and flagged — it must never reach settlement."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"5 consecutive failed login attempts must lock the account.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Security integrity constraint — if the system fails to lock after 5 attempts, brute-force attacks become trivially possible on a financial platform. The lock threshold could be policy, but the locking mechanism itself is an integrity requirement.","testable_assertion":"After exactly 5 consecutive failed login attempts for a single account, the account status must transition to 'locked' and reject all subsequent authentication attempts."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"Only a support agent can unlock a locked account — no self-service unlock.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"If a compromised account could self-unlock, the lock mechanism is meaningless. Human review is required to verify the legitimate owner.","testable_assertion":"A locked account must have no API endpoint, UI flow, or automated process that transitions it to 'unlocked' without a support agent action."},{"id":"BR-9","rule":"A notification must be sent for any transaction over $1,000.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Notification threshold is a business preference, not a data integrity or regulatory invariant. The dollar amount and notification channel are tunable.","testable_assertion":"When a transaction with amount > $1,000 is processed, exactly one notification must be dispatched to the account holder. Transactions at or below $1,000 must not trigger this notification."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"An unverified account with a zero balance","when":"Another user sends $200 to this account","then":"The transfer succeeds and the account balance increases by $200"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"An unverified account with a $200 balance","when":"The account holder attempts to send $50 to another user","then":"The transfer is rejected with a clear error indicating KYC completion is required"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"An unverified account with a $200 balance","when":"The account holder attempts to withdraw $50","then":"The withdrawal is rejected with a clear error indicating KYC completion is required"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A verified account that has already transferred $4,800 today","when":"The user initiates a $300 transfer","then":"The transfer is rejected because it would exceed the $5,000 daily limit; the user is shown the remaining available amount ($200)"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A partially-verified account that has transferred $0 today","when":"The user initiates a $500 transfer","then":"The transfer succeeds as it equals the daily limit exactly"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A partially-verified account that has transferred $400 today","when":"The user initiates a $200 transfer","then":"The transfer is rejected because it would exceed the $500 daily limit"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A transaction initiated yesterday that has now reached T+1 settlement","when":"The sender requests a reversal","then":"The reversal is rejected with an explanation that settled transactions are final"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A transaction initiated minutes ago that has not yet settled","when":"The sender requests a reversal","then":"The reversal is processed successfully (pre-settlement window)"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A verified user attempting to send $1,000 to a recipient","when":"The recipient matches an entry on the sanctions list","then":"The transfer is blocked immediately, never debited from sender, and the event is logged for compliance review"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"An account with 4 consecutive failed login attempts","when":"A 5th consecutive failed login attempt occurs","then":"The account is locked immediately; subsequent login attempts (even with correct credentials) are rejected until a support agent unlocks it"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"A locked account","when":"The account holder attempts to reset their password via self-service","then":"The password reset does not unlock the account; it remains locked until support agent intervention"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"A locked account","when":"A support agent performs the unlock action","then":"The account transitions to active and the user can log in with valid credentials"},{"id":"AC-13","given":"A verified user with sufficient balance and daily limit headroom","when":"The user sends a $1,500 transfer","then":"The transfer succeeds AND a notification is sent to the user"},{"id":"AC-14","given":"A verified user with sufficient balance and daily limit headroom","when":"The user sends a $999 transfer","then":"The transfer succeeds and NO high-value transaction notification is sent"},{"id":"AC-15","given":"An account with 4 consecutive failed logins followed by 1 successful login","when":"The user subsequently fails 3 more logins","then":"The account is NOT locked because the consecutive failure count was reset by the successful login"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Partially-verified account attempts to withdraw (not transfer)","expected_behavior":"Ambiguous — spec says unverified cannot withdraw, and mentions partially-verified transfer limits, but does not explicitly state whether partially-verified accounts can withdraw. Must be clarified."},{"scenario":"Transfer amount is exactly $1,000","expected_behavior":"No notification sent — the rule states 'over $1,000', meaning the threshold is exclusive. $1,000.00 exactly does not trigger a notification."},{"scenario":"Daily limit boundary at midnight — timezone ambiguity","expected_behavior":"The spec does not define what 'daily' means: calendar day in UTC, user's local timezone, or rolling 24-hour window. Must be defined to prevent limit gaming across timezone boundaries."},{"scenario":"Recipient becomes sanctioned between transaction initiation and settlement","expected_behavior":"The transaction should still be blocked or frozen pre-settlement. Sanctions screening should occur at both initiation and settlement to catch list updates."},{"scenario":"Multiple simultaneous transfers that individually fit within daily limit but collectively exceed it","expected_behavior":"The system must enforce limits atomically — concurrent requests must not allow the aggregate to exceed the daily cap. Requires serialized or optimistic-locking enforcement."},{"scenario":"Account locked during an active session","expected_behavior":"If 5 failed logins occur via API while a valid session exists in another client, the spec doesn't clarify whether existing sessions are terminated. Best practice: terminate all active sessions on lock."},{"scenario":"Transfer of exactly $0.00 or negative amount","expected_behavior":"Must be rejected — zero or negative transfers are invalid regardless of verification status. Standard financial validation."},{"scenario":"Partially-verified account receives a transfer of $1,500","expected_behavior":"Notification should still be sent — the $1,000 notification rule says 'any transaction', which should include inbound transfers regardless of verification tier."},{"scenario":"Self-transfer (send to own account)","expected_behavior":"Should still count against daily transfer limits and still undergo sanctions screening on the destination. May or may not be allowed as a product decision."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Incorrect daily limit enforcement could result in unauthorized fund movement exceeding risk tolerance. Sanctions violations carry severe fines ($250K–$1M+ per violation from OFAC). Settlement reversal bugs could cause ledger discrepancies requiring costly reconciliation.","operational":"Account locking mechanism directly impacts support team volume — too sensitive and support is overwhelmed; too lax and fraud risk increases. KYC tier management adds verification workflow complexity. Sanctions screening adds latency to every transfer and requires ongoing list maintenance.","user":"Verification requirements create onboarding friction — users cannot send money immediately. Account locking after 5 attempts may frustrate legitimate users who mistype passwords. Clear error messaging on limit rejections is critical to user trust.","risk":"High regulatory risk if sanctions screening has gaps or false negatives. Compliance risk if KYC enforcement can be bypassed. Financial crime risk if account locking is circumventable. Data integrity risk if settlement finality is not enforced atomically.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"End Users (Wallet Holders)","impact":"Must complete KYC to access full functionality; subject to daily limits, account locking, and transaction notifications. Experience is directly shaped by how clearly limits and errors are communicated."},{"group":"Compliance / Legal Team","impact":"Owns sanctions list maintenance and KYC policy. Needs audit trails for blocked sanctions transfers and account lock events. Bears regulatory liability if rules are not enforced."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Sole authority to unlock accounts — volume depends on lock frequency. Needs tooling to verify identity before unlocking. Must handle disputes about settled transactions they cannot reverse."},{"group":"Finance / Treasury","impact":"Settlement finality affects reconciliation. Daily limits affect cash flow forecasting. Ledger integrity depends on atomic enforcement of all transfer rules."},{"group":"Product / Growth","impact":"KYC friction impacts conversion rates. Partially-verified tier is a growth lever if well-defined — lets users experience the product before full verification."}]},"confidence":{"level":52,"reasoning":"The spec is mostly clear on individual rules but introduces the 'partially-verified' account tier without defining it, creating interpretive ambiguity about what verification steps it requires and exactly which capabilities it unlocks. The relationship between BR-1 ('must complete KYC to send') and BR-4 ('$500 limit for partially-verified') appears contradictory unless partially-verified is explicitly carved out as a tier that CAN send. Additionally, 'daily' is undefined temporally, and several edge cases around withdrawal rights for partial verification are unaddressed.","high_confidence_areas":["Settlement finality (T+1) is clearly defined and unambiguous","Sanctions blocking is a clear hard invariant","Account locking mechanism (5 attempts, support-only unlock) is well-specified","Notification threshold for transactions over $1,000 is clear","Unverified accounts: receive-only is clearly stated"],"low_confidence_areas":["Definition and capabilities of 'partially-verified' accounts — spec introduces the tier only in the context of daily limits without defining what partial verification means or whether it unlocks send/withdraw","Whether 'daily' means calendar day (and in which timezone) or rolling 24-hour window","Whether notifications apply to inbound transactions or only outbound","Whether existing sessions are terminated when an account is locked","Sanctions screening frequency — at initiation only, or also at settlement"],"assumptions":["Partially-verified is a distinct third tier between unverified and verified that permits sending with a reduced daily limit — otherwise the $500 limit rule is meaningless","A successful login resets the consecutive failed attempt counter to zero","The $1,000 notification threshold is exclusive (strictly greater than, not greater-than-or-equal)","Settlement at T+1 means one business day after transaction initiation","Sanctions screening occurs at minimum at transaction initiation time"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The spec states 'Users must complete identity verification (KYC) before they can send money' and 'Unverified accounts cannot send or withdraw', but then introduces a $500 daily transfer limit for 'partially-verified accounts'. This implies partially-verified accounts CAN send money despite KYC not being fully completed. The term 'partially-verified' is never defined — what verification steps does it require? Can it withdraw as well as send? This ambiguity affects the entire account capability matrix.","affected_stakeholders":["End Users","Compliance/Legal","Product/Growth","Engineering"],"options":["A) Partially-verified is a formal tier: has completed basic ID check but not full KYC. Can send up to $500/day but cannot withdraw.","B) Partially-verified can both send and withdraw up to $500/day — 'complete KYC' in BR-1 means completing at least partial verification.","C) The $500 limit is a drafting error — only fully verified accounts can send, making the partially-verified limit dead letter."],"recommendation":"Option A — it aligns with industry norms where tiered KYC (e.g., basic ID upload vs. full document + address verification) unlocks progressive capabilities. Explicitly define the three tiers and their exact capability matrix before implementation."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"'Daily transfer limits' does not specify what 'daily' means — calendar day in UTC, calendar day in the user's local timezone, or a rolling 24-hour window. This directly affects limit enforcement and can be exploited by users near day boundaries.","affected_stakeholders":["End Users","Finance/Treasury","Engineering"],"options":["A) Calendar day in UTC — simple to implement, consistent globally, but confusing for users in distant timezones.","B) Calendar day in user's local timezone — user-friendly but complex to enforce, especially for users who travel.","C) Rolling 24-hour window — most precise but computationally expensive and harder to explain to users."],"recommendation":"Option A (UTC calendar day) for implementation simplicity and auditability, with clear UX showing the user when their limit resets. This is the most common approach in fintech."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Digital wallet platform enabling money transfers between users, subject to regulatory compliance (KYC/AML), fraud prevention, and transaction integrity controls. The product operates in a regulated financial services space where identity verification tiers govern account capabilities and transfer limits.","bounded_context":"Digital Wallet — Account Lifecycle & Transaction Processing","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"KYC (Know Your Customer)","definition":"I

content.thinking:
Let me analyze this digital wallet domain specification carefully.

The spec is fairly clear. Let me identify any issues:

1. "partially-verified accounts" have a $500 limit but the main rule says "Unverified accounts can receive money but cannot send or withdraw." So what's a partially-verified account? That's an ambiguity - there are three states implied (unverified, partially-verified, verified) but the rules for partially-verified vs unverified aren't fully clear. Can partially-verified send money? The $500 limit implies yes, but the rule says only verified can send.

Actually wait - "Users must complete identity verification (KYC) before they can send money" and "Unverified accounts can receive money but cannot send or withdraw" - these talk about unverified. 

The transfer limits mention partially-verified accounts with a $500 cap, which suggests they can send money, yet the earlier rules state you need full KYC verification to send anything. This creates a contradiction - either partially-verified accounts can send (contradicting the KYC requirement) or they can't (making the $500 limit meaningless). The spec introduces this third account state without clarifying what it actually means or how it fits into the verification hierarchy.

Can send up to $500/day but cannot withdraw.","B) Partially-verified can both send and withdraw up to $500/day — 'complete KYC' in BR-1 means completing at least partial verification.","C) The $500 limit is a drafting error — only fully verified accounts can send, making the partially-verified limit dead letter."],"recommendation":"Option A — it aligns with industry norms where tiered KYC (e.g., basic ID upload vs. full document + address verification) unlocks progressive capabilities. Explicitly define the three tiers and their exact capability matrix before implementation."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"'Daily transfer limits' does not specify what 'daily' means — calendar day in UTC, calendar day in the user's local timezone, or a rolling 24-hour window. This directly affects limit enforcement and can be exploited by users near day boundaries.","affected_stakeholders":["End Users","Finance/Treasury","Engineering"],"options":["A) Calendar day in UTC — simple to implement, consistent globally, but confusing for users in distant timezones.","B) Calendar day in user's local timezone — user-friendly but complex to enforce, especially for users who travel.","C) Rolling 24-hour window — most precise but computationally expensive and harder to explain to users."],"recommendation":"Option A (UTC calendar day) for implementation simplicity and auditability, with clear UX showing the user when their limit resets. This is the most common approach in fintech."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}

trace.result: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Digital wallet platform enabling money transfers between users, subject to regulatory compliance (KYC/AML), fraud prevention, and transaction integrity controls. The product operates in a regulated financial services space where identity verification tiers govern account capabilities and transfer limits.","bounded_context":"Digital Wallet — Account Lifecycle & Transaction Processing","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"KYC (Know Your Customer)","definition":"Identity verification process that determines an account's capability tier. Completion unlocks full send/withdraw privileges."},{"term":"Unverified Account","definition":"An account that has not begun or completed any identity verification. Can receive funds only — cannot send or withdraw."},{"term":"Partially-Verified Account","definition":"An account that has completed some but not all KYC steps. Implied to have limited send capability with a $500 daily transfer limit, though exact capabilities are ambiguous in the spec."},{"term":"Verified Account","definition":"An account that has fully completed KYC. Has full send/withdraw/receive capabilities with a $5,000 daily transfer limit."},{"term":"Daily Transfer Limit","definition":"Maximum aggregate outbound transfer amount within a rolling or calendar day, determined by account verification tier."},{"term":"Settlement (T+1)","definition":"The point at which a transaction becomes final and irreversible — one business day after initiation."},{"term":"Sanctioned Entity","definition":"A person, organization, or jurisdiction on a regulatory sanctions list (e.g., OFAC). All transfers involving sanctioned entities must be blocked."},{"term":"Account Lock","definition":"A security state triggered by 5 consecutive failed login attempts. Locked accounts cannot authenticate until a support agent manually unlocks them."},{"term":"High-Value Transaction Notification","definition":"An alert sent to the user for any single transaction exceeding $1,000."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Users must complete KYC (identity verification) before they can send money.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Regulatory requirement — allowing unverified users to send funds violates AML/KYC regulations and exposes the platform to legal liability. System integrity depends on this gate.","testable_assertion":"An account with verification_status = 'unverified' must be rejected from initiating any outbound transfer."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Unverified accounts can receive money but cannot send or withdraw.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Receiving is low-risk from a regulatory standpoint, but sending/withdrawing without identity verification is a compliance violation. This is a hard regulatory gate.","testable_assertion":"An inbound transfer to an unverified account must succeed; an outbound transfer or withdrawal from an unverified account must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Daily transfer limit is $5,000 for verified accounts.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Limit thresholds are business policy — a VP or compliance officer could adjust this number based on risk appetite or market conditions.","testable_assertion":"The sum of all outbound transfers for a verified account within one day must not exceed $5,000. Any transfer that would breach this limit must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Daily transfer limit is $500 for partially-verified accounts.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Policy-driven threshold, adjustable by business decision. The existence of the tier itself is policy, not a physical or integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"The sum of all outbound transfers for a partially-verified account within one day must not exceed $500. Any transfer that would breach this limit must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"A transaction cannot be reversed once it has been settled (T+1).","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Settlement finality is a core financial integrity invariant. Reversing settled transactions would corrupt ledger state and violate accounting principles.","testable_assertion":"Any reversal or chargeback request for a transaction with status = 'settled' must be rejected by the system. Only pre-settlement transactions may be reversed."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Transfers to sanctioned entities must be blocked automatically before execution.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Legal/regulatory mandate — processing a transfer to a sanctioned entity is a criminal offense. This can never be overridden by any business policy.","testable_assertion":"Before any transfer is executed, the recipient must be screened against the sanctions list. If matched, the transfer must be blocked and flagged — it must never reach settlement."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"5 consecutive failed login attempts must lock the account.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Security integrity constraint — if the system fails to lock after 5 attempts, brute-force attacks become trivially possible on a financial platform. The lock threshold could be policy, but the locking mechanism itself is an integrity requirement.","testable_assertion":"After exactly 5 consecutive failed login attempts for a single account, the account status must transition to 'locked' and reject all subsequent authentication attempts."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"Only a support agent can unlock a locked account — no self-service unlock.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"If a compromised account could self-unlock, the lock mechanism is meaningless. Human review is required to verify the legitimate owner.","testable_assertion":"A locked account must have no API endpoint, UI flow, or automated process that transitions it to 'unlocked' without a support agent action."},{"id":"BR-9","rule":"A notification must be sent for any transaction over $1,000.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Notification threshold is a business preference, not a data integrity or regulatory invariant. The dollar amount and notification channel are tunable.","testable_assertion":"When a transaction with amount > $1,000 is processed, exactly one notification must be dispatched to the account holder. Transactions at or below $1,000 must not trigger this notification."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"An unverified account with a zero balance","when":"Another user sends $200 to this account","then":"The transfer succeeds and the account balance increases by $200"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"An unverified account with a $200 balance","when":"The account holder attempts to send $50 to another user","then":"The transfer is rejected with a clear error indicating KYC completion is required"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"An unverified account with a $200 balance","when":"The account holder attempts to withdraw $50","then":"The withdrawal is rejected with a clear error indicating KYC completion is required"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A verified account that has already transferred $4,800 today","when":"The user initiates a $300 transfer","then":"The transfer is rejected because it would exceed the $5,000 daily limit; the user is shown the remaining available amount ($200)"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A partially-verified account that has transferred $0 today","when":"The user initiates a $500 transfer","then":"The transfer succeeds as it equals the daily limit exactly"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A partially-verified account that has transferred $400 today","when":"The user initiates a $200 transfer","then":"The transfer is rejected because it would exceed the $500 daily limit"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A transaction initiated yesterday that has now reached T+1 settlement","when":"The sender requests a reversal","then":"The reversal is rejected with an explanation that settled transactions are final"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A transaction initiated minutes ago that has not yet settled","when":"The sender requests a reversal","then":"The reversal is processed successfully (pre-settlement window)"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A verified user attempting to send $1,000 to a recipient","when":"The recipient matches an entry on the sanctions list","then":"The transfer is blocked immediately, never debited from sender, and the event is logged for compliance review"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"An account with 4 consecutive failed login attempts","when":"A 5th consecutive failed login attempt occurs","then":"The account is locked immediately; subsequent login attempts (even with correct credentials) are rejected until a support agent unlocks it"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"A locked account","when":"The account holder attempts to reset their password via self-service","then":"The password reset does not unlock the account; it remains locked until support agent intervention"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"A locked account","when":"A support agent performs the unlock action","then":"The account transitions to active and the user can log in with valid credentials"},{"id":"AC-13","given":"A verified user with sufficient balance and daily limit headroom","when":"The user sends a $1,500 transfer","then":"The transfer succeeds AND a notification is sent to the user"},{"id":"AC-14","given":"A verified user with sufficient balance and daily limit headroom","when":"The user sends a $999 transfer","then":"The transfer succeeds and NO high-value transaction notification is sent"},{"id":"AC-15","given":"An account with 4 consecutive failed logins followed by 1 successful login","when":"The user subsequently fails 3 more logins","then":"The account is NOT locked because the consecutive failure count was reset by the successful login"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Partially-verified account attempts to withdraw (not transfer)","expected_behavior":"Ambiguous — spec says unverified cannot withdraw, and mentions partially-verified transfer limits, but does not explicitly state whether partially-verified accounts can withdraw. Must be clarified."},{"scenario":"Transfer amount is exactly $1,000","expected_behavior":"No notification sent — the rule states 'over $1,000', meaning the threshold is exclusive. $1,000.00 exactly does not trigger a notification."},{"scenario":"Daily limit boundary at midnight — timezone ambiguity","expected_behavior":"The spec does not define what 'daily' means: calendar day in UTC, user's local timezone, or rolling 24-hour window. Must be defined to prevent limit gaming across timezone boundaries."},{"scenario":"Recipient becomes sanctioned between transaction initiation and settlement","expected_behavior":"The transaction should still be blocked or frozen pre-settlement. Sanctions screening should occur at both initiation and settlement to catch list updates."},{"scenario":"Multiple simultaneous transfers that individually fit within daily limit but collectively exceed it","expected_behavior":"The system must enforce limits atomically — concurrent requests must not allow the aggregate to exceed the daily cap. Requires serialized or optimistic-locking enforcement."},{"scenario":"Account locked during an active session","expected_behavior":"If 5 failed logins occur via API while a valid session exists in another client, the spec doesn't clarify whether existing sessions are terminated. Best practice: terminate all active sessions on lock."},{"scenario":"Transfer of exactly $0.00 or negative amount","expected_behavior":"Must be rejected — zero or negative transfers are invalid regardless of verification status. Standard financial validation."},{"scenario":"Partially-verified account receives a transfer of $1,500","expected_behavior":"Notification should still be sent — the $1,000 notification rule says 'any transaction', which should include inbound transfers regardless of verification tier."},{"scenario":"Self-transfer (send to own account)","expected_behavior":"Should still count against daily transfer limits and still undergo sanctions screening on the destination. May or may not be allowed as a product decision."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Incorrect daily limit enforcement could result in unauthorized fund movement exceeding risk tolerance. Sanctions violations carry severe fines ($250K–$1M+ per violation from OFAC). Settlement reversal bugs could cause ledger discrepancies requiring costly reconciliation.","operational":"Account locking mechanism directly impacts support team volume — too sensitive and support is overwhelmed; too lax and fraud risk increases. KYC tier management adds verification workflow complexity. Sanctions screening adds latency to every transfer and requires ongoing list maintenance.","user":"Verification requirements create onboarding friction — users cannot send money immediately. Account locking after 5 attempts may frustrate legitimate users who mistype passwords. Clear error messaging on limit rejections is critical to user trust.","risk":"High regulatory risk if sanctions screening has gaps or false negatives. Compliance risk if KYC enforcement can be bypassed. Financial crime risk if account locking is circumventable. Data integrity risk if settlement finality is not enforced atomically.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"End Users (Wallet Holders)","impact":"Must complete KYC to access full functionality; subject to daily limits, account locking, and transaction notifications. Experience is directly shaped by how clearly limits and errors are communicated."},{"group":"Compliance / Legal Team","impact":"Owns sanctions list maintenance and KYC policy. Needs audit trails for blocked sanctions transfers and account lock events. Bears regulatory liability if rules are not enforced."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Sole authority to unlock accounts — volume depends on lock frequency. Needs tooling to verify identity before unlocking. Must handle disputes about settled transactions they cannot reverse."},{"group":"Finance / Treasury","impact":"Settlement finality affects reconciliation. Daily limits affect cash flow forecasting. Ledger integrity depends on atomic enforcement of all transfer rules."},{"group":"Product / Growth","impact":"KYC friction impacts conversion rates. Partially-verified tier is a growth lever if well-defined — lets users experience the product before full verification."}]},"confidence":{"level":52,"reasoning":"The spec is mostly clear on individual rules but introduces the 'partially-verified' account tier without defining it, creating interpretive ambiguity about what verification steps it requires and exactly which capabilities it unlocks. The relationship between BR-1 ('must complete KYC to send') and BR-4 ('$500 limit for partially-verified') appears contradictory unless partially-verified is explicitly carved out as a tier that CAN send. Additionally, 'daily' is undefined temporally, and several edge cases around withdrawal rights for partial verification are unaddressed.","high_confidence_areas":["Settlement finality (T+1) is clearly defined and unambiguous","Sanctions blocking is a clear hard invariant","Account locking mechanism (5 attempts, support-only unlock) is well-specified","Notification threshold for transactions over $1,000 is clear","Unverified accounts: receive-only is clearly stated"],"low_confidence_areas":["Definition and capabilities of 'partially-verified' accounts — spec introduces the tier only in the context of daily limits without defining what partial verification means or whether it unlocks send/withdraw","Whether 'daily' means calendar day (and in which timezone) or rolling 24-hour window","Whether notifications apply to inbound transactions or only outbound","Whether existing sessions are terminated when an account is locked","Sanctions screening frequency — at initiation only, or also at settlement"],"assumptions":["Partially-verified is a distinct third tier between unverified and verified that permits sending with a reduced daily limit — otherwise the $500 limit rule is meaningless","A successful login resets the consecutive failed attempt counter to zero","The $1,000 notification threshold is exclusive (strictly greater than, not greater-than-or-equal)","Settlement at T+1 means one business day after transaction initiation","Sanctions screening occurs at minimum at transaction initiation time"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The spec states 'Users must complete identity verification (KYC) before they can send money' and 'Unverified accounts cannot send or withdraw', but then introduces a $500 daily transfer limit for 'partially-verified accounts'. This implies partially-verified accounts CAN send money despite KYC not being fully completed. The term 'partially-verified' is never defined — what verification steps does it require? Can it withdraw as well as send? This ambiguity affects the entire account capability matrix.","affected_stakeholders":["End Users","Compliance/Legal","Product/Growth","Engineering"],"options":["A) Partially-verified is a formal tier: has completed basic ID check but not full KYC. Can send up to $500/day but cannot withdraw.","B) Partially-verified can both send and withdraw up to $500/day — 'complete KYC' in BR-1 means completing at least partial verification.","C) The $500 limit is a drafting error — only fully verified accounts can send, making the partially-verified limit dead letter."],"recommendation":"Option A — it aligns with industry norms where tiered KYC (e.g., basic ID upload vs. full document + address verification) unlocks progressive capabilities. Explicitly define the three tiers and their exact capability matrix before implementation."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"'Daily transfer limits' does not specify what 'daily' means — calendar day in UTC, calendar day in the user's local timezone, or a rolling 24-hour window. This directly affects limit enforcement and can be exploited by users near day boundaries.","affected_stakeholders":["End Users","Finance/Treasury","Engineering"],"options":["A) Calendar day in UTC — simple to implement, consistent globally, but confusing for users in distant timezones.","B) Calendar day in user's local timezone — user-friendly but complex to enforce, especially for users who travel.","C) Rolling 24-hour window — most precise but computationally expensive and harder to explain to users."],"recommendation":"Option A (UTC calendar day) for implementation simplicity and auditability, with clear UX showing the user when their limit resets. This is the most common approach in fintech."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
